M05 brake zones (rows ordered Piastri first): Piastri=#FF8000 (base papaya), Norris=#FFAC59; M14 wet crossover and R03 position chart (Norris first): Norris=#FF8000, Piastri=#FFAC59 — the same pair swaps colors between cards in one session. Mercedes flips too: R03/s303 position charts have Antonelli=#27F4D2 (base) / Russell=#73F8E2, while s303_wet_crossover has Russell=#27F4D2 / Antonelli=#73F8E2.
confirmed.suspected_root_cause: getDistinctTeamColors assigns base/shade by first-encounter order in the result rows instead of a stable per-driver key (e.g. driver number or alphabetical within team), so cross-card color identity for teammates is nondeterministic.
member_titles: Teammate base-vs-shade color assignment flips between cards depending on row order
confirmed.verdict_reasoning: Core claim verified, with one evidence-location caveat: the capture_*/ payloads contain no hex colors, but the audit_out/*.json spec artifacts in the same scratchpad reproduce the instability exactly — Mercedes: R03, s101_position_changes and s303_position_changes assign Antonelli=#27F4D2 (base) / Russell=#73F8E2 (shade), while s303_wet_crossover assigns Russell=#27F4D2 / Antonelli=#73F8E2, a flip between two cards of the same s303 session; McLaren: M14 gives Norris=#FF8000 / Piastri=#FFAC59 while the detector_report records Piastri=#FF8000 / Norris=#FFAC59 elsewhere. The finding's M05 phrasing is slightly loose (M05's spec is a single diverging 'Piastri − Norris' series using #FF8000/#FFAC59 as positive/negative rather than two per-driver series), but the cross-card flip class is unambiguous, and the finding correctly notes the color-collision class does NOT reproduce. Code confirms the mechanism at web/src/lib/f1-team-colors.ts:107-118: the first driver encountered per team keeps the base color and subsequent teammates get lightened variants, keyed purely on pass order. Design lens: the docstring says 'Order-stable: colors are assigned in the order names are passed' — documenting within-call determinism, not endorsing nondeterministic cross-card teammate identity; no sign-off or test covers cross-card stability. P3: cosmetic cross-card inconsistency with no wrong data.

confirmed.title: /api/track-outline derives outlines from location telemetry in ~14.5s cold against the shared 15s statement timeout, with only an in-process cache — zero headroom on first hit per circuit
confirmed.evidence: Exact params from the only client-fetch spec in the capture (track_corner_delta, circuit='Jeddah', no sessionKey — matching useTrackOutline(chart.circuit) in track-corner-delta.tsx): curl 'http://localhost:3000/api/track-outline?circuit=Jeddah' returned HTTP 200 in 14.535s cold with a complete payload (358 points, corners, sectors [0.3302,0.6634], 4 drsZones — every field TrackMap dereferences), then 0.008s warm from cache. The response contract is healthy; the margin is not: the outline derivation query runs on the same pool whose statement_timeout is 15000ms (db/driver.ts:51), so on the exact DB-slow days documented in finding template_exec.perf... the client fetch would return null and TrackCornerDelta/TrackSpeedMap/MinisectorStrip silently drop the track ribbon (useTrackOutline maps any non-ok/failed fetch to null, track-map.tsx:61-68). No track_speed_map/telemetry_overlay specs were emitted in this capture to test their exact params — all three producers fell to the timeout fallback — which itself narrows client-fetch audit coverage.
confirmed.suspected_root_cause: Track-outline derivation from location telemetry is a ~14-15s cold query with only an in-process cache; it shares the 15s statement timeout with the chat pipeline, leaving effectively zero headroom on first hit per circuit per server process.
member_titles: /api/track-outline cold fetch takes 14.5s against the same pool's 15s statement timeout — first render of any track-ribbon chart is one hiccup from failing
confirmed.verdict_reasoning: Reproduced live during verification: curl 'http://localhost:3000/api/track-outline?circuit=Jeddah' returned HTTP 200 in 14.96s cold and 0.007s on the immediate warm repeat — matching the audit's 14.5s/0.008s measurement almost exactly. Code confirms the mechanics: route.ts:45 uses an in-process Map cache (outlineCache) keyed per circuit/session/driver/channels with only a browser-side Cache-Control header, so every fresh server process pays the full derivation; the derivation runs on the db/driver.ts pool whose statement_timeout defaults to 15000ms (OPENF1_QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS); useTrackOutline (track-map.tsx:61-68) maps any non-ok/failed fetch to null so the track ribbon silently disappears on failure. One nuance the finding slightly overstates: the ~15s wall time spans several sequential statements (core.laps_enriched refLap scan, raw.location window, raw.car_data window, DRS/corner queries), so no single statement is literally at 14.5s — but the laps_enriched scan is the same unmaterialized-view query class that blew the identical 15s budget on the chat path the same day, so the one-hiccup-from-null risk is real. Design lens: the client-side-fetch spec design is signed off and the null fallback is intentional graceful degradation, but the near-timeout cold path is neither documented nor ticketed. P3 (perf/polish) is the right level: degradation is silent but graceful, and only first-hit-per-circuit-per-process is exposed.

refuted.title: f1.track_segments is populated only with corner_analysis-covered corners for a subset of circuits; outline/telemetry APIs and TrackMap treat it as the complete corner catalog, serving empty or near-empty corner sets
refuted.evidence:
GET /api/track-outline?circuit=Sakhir&sessionKey=10014&driver=1&channels=1 → 200, 361 points all with speed/throttle/brake, sectors [0.3194,0.721], 4 DRS zones, zero malformed points (endpoint healthy) — but corners = only [Turn 1, Turn 8, Turn 10] for a 15-corner circuit; Spa returns 4 (Eau Rouge, Raidillon, Pouhon, Stavelot) of 19. route.ts:269-278 reads corners from f1.track_segments WHERE segment_kind='corner'. The full-track speed map (M04-class card) renders with only 3 corner labels; also TrackMap's corner-number extraction (track-map.tsx:173 `c.label.match(/\d+/)?.[0] ?? c.label`) falls back to the FULL NAME for named corners, drawing 26px 'Eau Rouge'/'Stavelot' strings where single digits are expected.
---
Sequential curls against the running dev server: /api/lap-telemetry?sessionKey=9888&drivers=10,27 → 200 with both drivers' full channel arrays but corners: 0; sessionKey=9869&drivers=31,10 → 200, corners: 0; /api/track-outline?circuit=Austin|Interlagos|Singapore|Yas%20Marina%20Circuit → 200 with healthy points (277-371) and sectors fractions but corners: 0; circuit=Suzuka → corners: 7. Both routes read corner labels from f1.track_segments WHERE circuit_short_name=$1 AND segment_kind='corner' (track-outline/route.ts:269-274, lap-telemetry/route.ts:138). telemetry-overlay-chart.tsx falls back to percent ticks when cornerTicks is empty, so degradation is graceful — but the corner-anchored x-axis the overlay was designed around is missing at most venues. New aspect vs the known corner_analysis coverage note: this is the f1.track_segments table feeding the client-fetch chart APIs, not analytics.corner_analysis.
refuted.suspected_root_cause: f1.track_segments is populated only with the corners that corner_analysis covers, not full circuit corner sets; the outline API and TrackMap treat it as the complete corner catalog.
member_titles: f1.track_segments carries only the analyzed corners (3 at Sakhir, 4 at Spa) so track maps label a handful of corners
f1.track_segments has no corner rows for most circuits — /api/track-outline and /api/lap-telemetry return corners: [] (Austin, Interlagos, Singapore, Yas Marina), dropping corner tick labels
refuted.verdict_reasoning: Evidence is real: saved artifacts show corners:[] for Interlagos/Mexico outlines and lap-telemetry session 9869, and only 7 corners for Suzuka; both routes read f1.track_segments WHERE segment_kind='corner' (track-outline/route.ts:271-272, lap-telemetry/route.ts:138-139) as claimed. But the sparse population is a documented scoping decision, not an unnoticed defect: diagnostic/phase_19_analytics_capability_plan_2026-05-02.md defines slice 20-track-segments-corners as 'Hand-curated FIA corner zones bolted into f1.track_segments; one-row update per corner', and diagnostic/phase25_target_grades.json already records the partial seed with explicit expansion escapes ('Expand the f1.track_segments ... seed to cover Zandvoort/Las Vegas'). Consumers deliberately degrade gracefully — telemetry-overlay-chart.tsx falls back to default percent ticks when cornerTicks is empty, which the finding itself concedes. The one genuinely new nugget is track-map.tsx:173's label fallback (c.label.match(/\d+/)?.[0] ?? c.label) drawing full names like 'Eau Rouge' where single-digit labels are expected — a rendering polish nit worth a one-line fix, which keeps this at P3 rather than a data defect.
